completed_tasks.phase: Competitive — Semantic Claim Verification v2 (Pinecone + cosine similarity hybrid)
completed_tasks.note: Cycle 63: Upgraded Claim Verification Engine from v1.0 to v2.0 with semantic similarity via Pinecone embeddings. (A) Enhanced crossReferenceClaim() in claims.ts: now uses hybrid semantic+full-text search. Generates OpenAI embeddings for claim text, queries Pinecone for top-15 similar signals (threshold 0.6), combines with PostgreSQL full-text keyword search. (B) New determineAgreement() function: computes cosine similarity between claim and snippet embeddings to classify agrees/disagrees. Uses SEMANTIC_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD (0.75) and SEMANTIC_CONTRADICTION_THRESHOLD (0.40) with 17 contradiction keyword signals (denied/refuted/debunked/etc). High similarity + contradiction = disagrees (semantically related but contradicting). (C) Updated verification status: now properly classifies disputed claims based on disagreeing source count. (D) Engine metadata upgraded: version 2.0.0, reports semantic enabled/disabled, thresholds. (E) Test suite: claims-semantic.test.ts — 38 test cases across 8 describe blocks (semantic constants, contradiction signals, Pinecone integration, agreement determination, cosine similarity, verification status, weighted scoring, engine metadata).
